narrative.headline: 2018 Dodge Durango: electrical and lighting issues lead owner complaints
narrative.summary: Across 226 owner complaints to NHTSA, the electrical system is by far the most-reported area at 86 reports (38%), followed by exterior lighting at 54 reports (24%). Engine and service brakes are tied at 25 reports each (11%), and seats account for 20 reports (9%). Most complaints did not involve a crash or injury. The service brakes category has the most crashes (3) and 1 injury; seats also report 1 injury.

issues.sample: THE ENTIRE 8.4" SCREEN ON THE DASHBOARD FLASHES ON AND OFF CONTINUALLY WHETHER THE VEHICLE IS RUNNING OR NOT.  SEEMS LIKE THERE IS A MAJOR SHORT IN THE ELECTRICAL SYSTEM. WHILE THIS ISSUE IS HAPPENING YOU HAVE NO CONTROL OVER HEATING, VENTILATION, RADIO, NAVIGATION, APPS OR APPLE
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Most-reported area: 86 complaints (38%), with 1 crash and no fires or injuries reported. Owners describe the dashboard touchscreen flashing on and off and losing control of climate, radio, and navigation.

issues.title: Exterior Lighting
issues.blurb: 54 complaints (24%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Owners describe intermittent electrical problems affecting the back-up camera, navigation, radio, and A/C.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 25 complaints (11%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Owners describe the center console controls locking up while driving until the vehicle is pulled over and shut off.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 25 complaints (11%), and the category with the most safety reports here: 3 crashes and 1 injury. One owner describes the vehicle suddenly accelerating while pulling slowly into a parking lot.

issues.title: Seats
issues.blurb: 20 complaints (9%), with 1 injury and no crashes or fires reported. One owner describes a seat folding or reclining unexpectedly.
